description: SpaceX is going public: S-1 filed with the SEC on 2026-05-20, pricing expected 2026-06-11 and Nasdaq listing 2026-06-12 under ticker SPCX. Targeting a raise of up to ~$75B (more than 2x the prior record IPO) at a valuation of at least $1.8T (early-investor consensus ~$2.0-2.2T) — the largest IPO in US market history by valuation. Up to ~30% of shares are reportedly allocated to retail investors (3x the typical 5-10%), enlarging the public float and the post-listing retail-flow dynamic. Starlink (10.3M subscribers as of end-March 2026, doubled YoY, ~58% of SpaceX revenue) is the valuation anchor. This is a liquidity + sentiment catalyst for the entire commercial-space complex: it creates the first directly investable large-cap space equity, forces active managers and index providers to take a sector view, and has already driven the proxy ETFs (UFO +75% YTD, ROKT +58% YTD; UFO AUM crossed $1B) sharply higher in anticipation. CAVEAT: SpaceX's advisers have flagged the listing could slip to 2027 depending on market conditions — the event_date is a target, not yet confirmed fired.
user_notes: Registered 2026-06-08 ahead of the IPO window at the user's request (the IPO is so close it could move sector news). This is the canonical 'event overhang' for entry_timing on space_commercial / space-adjacent reads (CLAUDE.md cites the SpaceX mega-IPO as the example). Overhang read is AMBIGUOUS: bullish as a sector-wide re-rating + liquidity event (relation 'confirms'), but a near-term risk for anyone entering the proxy ETFs right now — they have already rallied +75%/+58% YTD pricing it in (is_priced_in 0.50), so a 'sell-the-news' or a slip-to-2027 unwind is a live downside. POST-IPO ACTION: once listing is confirmed (or slips), update event_date/status; if it prices Jun 11-12, let it decay (30d half-life) and watch first-30-day trajectory (break below ~$1.4T = bear signal for sector ETF flows).
